title: Rich Fluffy Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: A rich and fluffy Moroccan dish featuring chicken, honey, and aromatic spices, perfect for a crowd-pleasing dinner. This recipe is sugar-free friendly and utilizes a pressure cooker for efficiency.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the pressure cooker over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger, cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken to the pressure cooker and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, and cinnamon, cook for 1 minute.
Add the honey, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper, stir to combine.
Close the lid of the pressure cooker and set the valve to sealing.
Pressure cook on high for 10 minutes, then allow the pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes.
Quick-release any remaining pressure, then open the lid and check the chicken for tenderness.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
